impact.why: The wrapper crypto/bls.AggregateSignatures is a 1-line pass-through to blst.AggregateSignatures. If the wrapper signature changes (param/return types), the backend it forwards to must change in lockstep, and vice-versa; they are a contract pair.

notes: CRITICAL FINDING: crypto/bls.AggregateSignatures has NO production callers. rg for 'bls.AggregateSignatures(' returns zero non-test hits; the only references to the (sibling) blst.AggregateSignatures are in crypto/bls/blst/test/*_test.go. The WBFT seal aggregation and verification paths (consensus/wbft/engine/engine.go aggregateSeal:141, verify:1388; AggregatePublicKeys:1355) use bls.AggregateCompressedSignatures and bls.AggregatePublicKeys — a DIFFERENT function operating on [][]byte compressed inputs — NOT AggregateSignatures. So a pure signature change to AggregateSignatures has essentially no production blast radius beyond keeping its blst delegate in sync. The seed itself is excluded. Test callers (blst/test/signature_test.go, bls_benchmark_test.go) excluded as pure _test.go. If the intent was the conceptual 'seal aggregation' function, the real seed should be AggregateCompressedSignatures, whose callers are engine.go:141 (aggregateSeal) and engine.go:1388 (verify path).
